headlines.title: Total repression and air strikes bring unrelenting dread for Iranians
headlines.description: Tehran residents tell the BBC they're caught between US-Israeli bombing and an Iranian regime trying to reassert its power.

headlines.title: Scotland's assisted dying bill rejected after emotional debate
headlines.description: Scotland would have become the first part of the UK to legalise the process had MSPs backed the proposals.

headlines.title: Morocco awarded Africa Cup of Nations title after Senegal win overturned
headlines.description: Morocco declared the winners of the 2025 Africa Cup of Nations after the Confederation of African Football overturns the result of their final defeat by Senegal.

headlines.title: Ukraine faces missile shortage due to Middle East war, says Zelensky
headlines.description: In an exclusive interview with the BBC, the Ukrainian president says Putin wants a "long war" between the US and Iran.

headlines.title: Higgs Boson breakthrough was UK triumph, but British physics faces 'catastrophic' cuts
headlines.description: Britain is preparing to cancel its contribution to one of the Large Hadron Collider's next major upgrades.

headlines.title: Rayner warns immigration reforms risk being 'un-British'
headlines.description: Most migrant workers will have to wait longer to qualify for permanent residence under the government's proposals.

headlines.title: Dog owners to face unlimited fines if their pets attack livestock under new law
headlines.description: Police will have new powers to seize and detain dogs that have attacked or chased farm animals.

headlines.title: Nigeria president begins first UK state visit in 37 years
headlines.description: King Charles and senior royals will provide a ceremonial welcome for Nigeria's President Tinubu on his state visit.

headlines.title: Ad for AI editing app which said it could 'remove anything' banned
headlines.description: The UK regulator said the ad condoned "digitally altering and exposing women's bodies without their consent."

headlines.title: Temperatures forecast to reach 20C in warmest day of the year so far
headlines.description: Temperatures are on the rise again and it will feel like spring has returned.

headlines.title: Why is MenB vaccine not given to teenagers in UK and should they be offered it?
headlines.description: Students and older teens have not been vaccinated against the strain that has caused the outbreak of cases in Kent.

headlines.title: Watch: Mother 'absolutely shocked' to find daughter had meningitis
headlines.description: Khali Goodwin's daughter Keeleigh is recovering after being admitted to hospital with meningitis.

headlines.title: Was response to outbreak too slow?
headlines.description: Questions are being asked about whether the NHS and authorities should have raised the alarm sooner.

headlines.title: Why the B-52s are trading their Love Shack for Downton Abbey
headlines.description: The new wave pranksters plan a final visit to the UK, with detours to their favourite TV show sets.

headlines.title: Scotland's assisted dying bill has fallen - what happens now?
headlines.description: Debate on the issue has been ongoing in parliament for more than 15 years but this is the closest legislation has made it to become a law.

headlines.title: The Papers: 'First cracks' in Trump camp over Iran war and UK tackles meningitis outbreak
headlines.description: The ongoing US-Israeli conflict with Iran and the meningitis outbreak in the UK lead Wednesday's papers.

headlines.title: Mean Girls actress says being cast as Regina George is 'monumental'
headlines.description: Vivian Panka is the first black actress to play the character full-time in in the UK production of Mean Girls.

headlines.title: I've applied for 500 jobs in two months since graduating
headlines.description: Charlotte Briggs has applied for hundreds of jobs and cannot find work, despite achieving a 2:1 degree.

headlines.title: Will Champions League be Guardiola's Man City regret?
headlines.description: Pep Guardiola is one of the greatest managers of all time, but will his Champions League record be a cause for frustration?

headlines.title: Air strike hit Kabul rehab centre as patients ate dinner, survivor tells BBC
headlines.description: The exact number killed in Monday night's strike by Pakistan is still not known, but is feared to be in the hundreds.

headlines.title: Death of Ali Larijani deepens crisis at heart of Iran's leadership
headlines.description: Larijani has long been seen as one of the Islamic Republic's most experienced and influential policy makers.

headlines.title: Trump says Nato making 'foolish mistake' over Iran
headlines.description: The US president expresses frustration that US allies are not helping secure the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.title: Fear and defiance in southern Lebanon city as Israel-Hezbollah war intensifies
headlines.description: Civilians who remain despite evacuation orders in Tyre – some unable to move, others defiant - are trying to continue their daily routines next to bomb sites.

headlines.title: What have US allies and China said about sending ships to the Strait of Hormuz?
headlines.description: US allies have either pushed back or remained cautious on Trump's initial request to send ships to the crucial oil route.

headlines.title: School phone bans not enough to cut screen time alone, researchers say
headlines.description: New research suggests efforts to suppress screen time need to go further than the school gates to be effective.

headlines.title: Blackpool, Ipswich, Milton Keynes among nine finalists for UK City of Culture 2029
headlines.description: The nine-strong longlist of cities will receive a £60,000 boost of funding to develop a full bid plan.

headlines.title: Government admits adoption system 'needs to improve' after BBC investigation highlights failures
headlines.description: The children and families minister for England apologises to families who have received "support from services that isn't good enough".

headlines.title: Suspected stray bullet from military drill hits girl at South Korea playground
headlines.description: South Korea's military has since halted all shooting drills involving small firearms.

headlines.title: Greyhound racing to be banned in Wales after Senedd vote
headlines.description: But the legislation faces a legal challenge and accusations ministers did not consult properly.

headlines.title: Dates confirmed for new driving test booking rules
headlines.description: Only learners will be able to book a car driving test under new rules to make the system fairer.

headlines.title: Watch: Sean Penn receives 'Oscar' in Ukraine after skipping US ceremony
headlines.description: The Academy Award winning US actor won his third Oscar on Sunday, but skipped the ceremony to visit Ukraine.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Starmer Turns His Attention To Ukraine (Or Tries To)
headlines.description: Zelenksy comes to parliament, as Trump hits out at Starmer (again).

headlines.title: The Global Story: How does war affect a child’s brain?
headlines.description: Veteran war reporter Fergal Keane on PTSD among children in conflicts worldwide.

headlines.title: Morocco awarded Afcon title after Senegal's win overturned
headlines.description: Morocco declared the winners of the 2025 Africa Cup of Nations after the Confederation of African Football overturns the result of their final defeat by Senegal.

headlines.title: Will Champions League be Guardiola's Man City regret?
headlines.description: Pep Guardiola is one of the greatest managers of all time, but will his Champions League record be a cause for frustration?

headlines.title: The 'anti-Lamine' backed to be one of best in world
headlines.description: Newcastle United defender Lewis Hall renews hostilities with Barcelona star Lamine Yamal on Wednesday night and team-mate Kieran Trippier believes he has "all the attributes to be one of the best left-backs in the world".

headlines.title: Dubai withdrawal criticism ridiculous - Sabalenka
headlines.description: World number one Aryna Sabalenka dismisses criticism of her late withdrawal from the Dubai Championships and says she may not play at the tournament again.

headlines.title: 'Bielle-Biarrey's pace spooked me' - Chessum on intercept try finish
headlines.description: England back row Ollie Chessum says the fear of being run down by Louis Bielle Biarrey prevented him from running in an intercept closer to the post in defeat by France.

headlines.title: Rice 'The Horse' epitomises relentless Arsenal
headlines.description: Declan Rice is called "The Horse" by his Arsenal team-mates and his performance in the 2-0 win against Bayer Leverkusen again showed why he has more than earned that nickname.
